perturbation.original_text: A Confidential Information@ shall mean any and all information that a Party may reasonably expect to remain confidential and not shared with the general public at any time or that information that is protected by law.
perturbation.changed_text: A Confidential Information@ shall mean only specific documents explicitly marked as confidential by the disclosing party, and solely to the extent such documents are not already publicly available.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of 'Confidential Information' is broad, encompassing any information reasonably expected to be confidential. The modified definition significantly narrows this scope to only documents explicitly marked confidential and not publicly available. This contradiction creates uncertainty because the original text implies a broader range of information should be protected, while the modification limits it only to explicitly marked and non-public items. This contradicts the initial intent of protecting a wider array of information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: It is anticipated that the Business Affiliate will provide services both for clients of the Business Affiliate and UDC that involve the use and disclosure of Confidential Information (the "Services"). Except as otherwise specified herein, the Parties may make any and all uses of Confidential Information necessary to perform the Services. Additionally, the Parties may disclose Confidential Information for the purposes authorized by this Agreement only (a) to its employees, subcontractors and agents, in accordance with this Agreement, or (b) as directed by the other Party. The Parties expressly agree that any and all uses or disclosures of the Confidential Information by a Party will be done in accordance with the terms of this Agreement and the provisions of all applicable federal and state laws and regulations.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'Confidential Information' broadly, encompassing information reasonably expected to be confidential. The changed text narrows this definition to only explicitly marked documents not publicly available. The contradicted text anticipates the use and disclosure of 'Confidential Information' in providing services. By limiting the definition of 'Confidential Information', the change creates a contradiction because it restricts the scope of information the parties are allowed to use and disclose, potentially hindering their ability to perform the services as originally intended. The parties may need to use or disclose information that falls under the original definition of confidential information but not the new, more restrictive one, creating a conflict.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall become effective on the Effective Date and shall continue in effect until either Party informs the other Party with thirty (30) day prior written notice of termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall become effective on the Effective Date and shall continue in effect indefinitely, with no possibility of termination by either party under any circumstances.
perturbation.explanation: The original text allows for termination with 30 days' notice, providing a clear mechanism for ending the agreement. The modified text states the agreement continues indefinitely with no possibility of termination. This creates a direct contradiction regarding the term and termination provisions of the agreement. It becomes unclear if the parties can ever end the agreement, leading to potential legal disputes if one party seeks to terminate against the stated terms.
perturbation.contradicted_text: UDC may immediately terminate this Agreement without notice and any related agreement if it determines that the Business Affiliate has breached a material provision of this Agreement or if the activities contemplated under this Agreement will result in a violation of any federal or state law or if such Services would, in the opinion of UDC and its counsel. Alternatively, UDC may choose to: (i) provide the Business Affiliate with ten (10) days written notice of the existence of an alleged material breach; and (ii) afford the Business Affiliate an opportunity to cure said alleged material breach upon mutually agreeable terms. Failure to cure in the manner set forth in this paragraph is grounds for the immediate termination of the Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The modified text explicitly states that the agreement continues indefinitely with no possibility of termination by either party under any circumstances. This directly contradicts the section that allows UDC to immediately terminate the agreement under certain conditions, such as a material breach by the Business Affiliate. The change removes the possibility of termination by either party, while the contradicted text explicitly states the possibility of immediate termination by UDC. This conflict makes the agreement's enforceability unclear, constituting a structural flaw that creates an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Parties hereby covenant and agree to indemnify and hold harmless each other, it's agents and representatives from and against any and all losses, costs, expenses, liabilities, claims, demands, judgments and its settlements of every nature that are actually incurred by a Party, including without limitation reasonable attorney's fees for pre-trial, trial, appellate, judgment, post-judgment, execution, and alternative dispute resolution, which arise out of any use or disclosure of Confidential Information not specifically permitted by this Agreement or arise out of the services rendered by Business Affiliate.
perturbation.changed_text: Neither party shall be required to indemnify the other for any reason, regardless of circumstances, and each party is solely responsible for all losses, costs, and liabilities it may incur.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates indemnification for unauthorized use/disclosure of Confidential Information and services provided by the Business Affiliate, providing a clear responsibility for each party. The modified text eliminates any indemnification obligation, stating each party is solely responsible for their own losses. This introduces a significant contradiction, as the original clause assigns specific responsibility for certain risks, while the modification removes this entirely. It creates uncertainty as to who bears the financial burden in case of breaches or liabilities covered in the original text, as now there isn't any responsibility.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Survival. The respective rights and obligations of Business Affiliate and UDC under the provisions of Sections 3(g) [Responsibilities of the Parties] and 3(h) [Responsibilities of the Parties] and 6 shall survive the termination of this Agreement indefinitely.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates indemnification, whereas the changed text eliminates it entirely, creating a direct conflict. The contradicted text, "Survival. The respective rights and obligations of Business Affiliate and UDC under the provisions of Sections 3(g) [Responsibilities of the Parties] and 3(h) [Responsibilities of the Parties] and 6 shall survive the termination of this Agreement indefinitely," explicitly mentions responsibilities and obligations that survive termination. The modified text's removal of indemnification directly contradicts these surviving responsibilities, leading to an in-text contradiction.
